_meta.notes: Canon back-fill per MER-51/D70. All 74 decisions (D01-D74), 7 canon flags, sessions 1-11, issues through MER-63.

decisions.summary: CAG as primary architecture candidate
decisions.rationale: Corpus size (~300K emails compiling to ~200 articles) fits within context window. CAG produces more consistent, coherent query results than RAG by eliminating the lossy retrieval step. pgvector and Voyage AI embeddings no longer required.

decisions.summary: GraphRAG as hybrid candidate to evaluate
decisions.rationale: Email corpus is inherently relational (people, companies, topics, decisions). GraphRAG captures relationship signal that vector search misses. Recommended: GraphRAG POC on 6-month email slice before scaling.
decisions.superseded_notes: Kuzu (recommended DB) acquired by Apple (D09). Architecture shifted to unified store (D04).

decisions.summary: Deep context methodology formalized as reusable skill
decisions.rationale: Session 33 from Perkin project served as reference spec. Skill created, packaged, delivered. Applicable across claude.ai, Claude Code, and Cowork.

decisions.summary: Unified store architecture: one store, many lenses
decisions.rationale: Multi-backend + router model has seven identified weaknesses: router is the hardest/most failure-prone component, most queries span backends, operational surface area grows with each backend, data consistency drifts between stores, ingestion quality is the upstream dependency that matters more than retrieval pattern, CAG staleness is unsolvable with separate stores, and upfront classification is brittle vs iterative agent reasoning. Unified architecture solves all seven. Supersedes D01 and D02.

decisions.summary: PostgreSQL+AGE as sole database
decisions.rationale: AGE provides Cypher graph queries as a Postgres extension. Combined with tsvector (FTS) and jsonb (document), Postgres becomes the multi-model canonical store. Eliminates separate graph database. ArcadeDB evaluated and rejected. Corpus scale (tens of thousands of nodes) doesn't require purpose-built graph engine.

decisions.summary: Zuplo as unified API/MCP layer
decisions.rationale: MCP Server Handler exposes retrieval lenses as agent-discoverable tools. AI gateway features (semantic caching, token-based rate limiting, model routing) solve cost control. Observability included. Edge deployment with GitOps. Scope narrowed by D72: Zuplo responsibility is unified HTTP gateway (auth, rate-limit, trace, policy pipeline). MCP hosting is NOT Zuplo's responsibility.

decisions.summary: Railway for infrastructure, Vercel only for frontend
decisions.rationale: Vercel can't run custom Docker containers or host persistent databases. Railway is the right platform for Postgres+AGE and API services. Vercel's role is limited to frontend/dashboard when that phase arrives.

decisions.summary: Project codename: Meridian
decisions.rationale: Reference line / single source of truth metaphor. Optics metaphor (lens focus). Clean repo name, clean namespace, no ecosystem collisions.

decisions.summary: Kuzu eliminated from consideration
decisions.rationale: Acquired by Apple October 2025, GitHub archived. Community forks (LadybugDB, Vela Partners) lack corporate backing and carry abandonment risk. Session 01 recommendation to use Kuzu for POC is invalidated.

decisions.summary: Schema and extraction prompt are model-agnostic
decisions.rationale: JSON contract defines output shape. Any model producing conforming JSON works. No architectural coupling to a specific LLM provider.

decisions.summary: Multi-model routing via Zuplo gateway
decisions.rationale: Model selection is a gateway policy, not application logic. Enables Haiku for bulk, Opus for complex, Gemini for long-context — all without code changes.
decisions.superseded_notes: D22 moves model selection to user-facing preference in the Meridian interface. Zuplo executes routing but does not decide it.

decisions.summary: Validate extraction quality against Haiku first
decisions.rationale: Haiku is the cost floor. If it handles extraction reliably, everything above it can too. Failures identify routing rules.

decisions.summary: No pre-filter on email ingestion
decisions.rationale: Automated notifications carry employment, financial, and temporal signal. Noise is a data engineering bias that conflicts with total context capture. Every email gets extracted.

decisions.summary: Source classification as metadata, not a filter
decisions.rationale: Emails tagged with source_type at extraction time (personal, notification, advertisement, transactional, newsletter, system). Classification is queryable metadata for lens filtering, not a gate.

decisions.summary: POC scope: 2024-2025 email subset
decisions.rationale: 143,996 files (~72K emails), 26 folders, 8.74 GB. Large enough to validate entity resolution, small enough to iterate. Estimated ~$55-80 at Haiku rates.

decisions.summary: Organization.org_type expanded with school and school_district
decisions.rationale: Humble ISD is governmental, not a Company. WLMS is a school. Existing org_type values don't cover educational institutions. School districts are the primary Private-domain organizational entity for family context.

decisions.summary: Extraction prompt: abbreviations kept verbatim
decisions.rationale: WLMS should not be expanded by the extraction model. Cross-email entity resolution handles abbreviation-to-full-name mapping. Guessing expansions introduces error.

decisions.summary: Name parsing heuristic for email-only contacts
decisions.rationale: When no display name and email follows firstname.lastname@domain.com, parse as Firstname Lastname (title-cased). Flag needs_review: true. Validated by johnson.marteba@gmail.com -> Marteba Johnson.

decisions.summary: Calendar invites forwarded by user = personal source_type
decisions.rationale: User-forwarded calendar invite is personal — interpersonal content even if delivery mechanism is automated. Notification reserved for system-generated alerts with no human-authored content.

decisions.summary: Triage interface is conversational, not clinical
decisions.rationale: Rejected approve/reject button queues. Triage must be a natural language conversation where Claude presents findings and asks questions, and corrections cascade across the graph.

decisions.summary: Three-function interface architecture
decisions.rationale: Function 1 (Conversational Triage), Function 2 (Insight Explorer), Function 3 (Execution Layer). Functions inform each other. F2 and F3 inform how F1 presents data. F3 is always available from F1 and F2. Recommendations engine spans all three.

decisions.summary: Model selection is user-facing preference (supersedes D11)
decisions.rationale: Model selection must be in the Meridian interface settings, changeable anytime, like Claude.ai's model picker. Zuplo executes routing but does not decide it. Per-task overrides supported. Cost estimation surfaced in UI. Provider-agnostic.

decisions.summary: Batch-first entity triage
decisions.rationale: Process 500 emails, stage results, present entities grouped by type. Entity-centric triage, not email-centric. More efficient: merge 47 instances of the same person at once instead of email-by-email.

decisions.summary: Extraction as proposal, human-in-the-loop is architectural
decisions.rationale: Extraction model cannot resolve ambiguity requiring life context. Extractions land in staging table, not live graph, until human review. Not a quality gate — a fundamental architectural requirement. Any system skipping this step is building on unreviewed assumptions.

decisions.summary: BSL 1.1 license with full IP enforcement
decisions.rationale: Public repo requires explicit IP protection. Permits viewing and non-production use. Reserves commercial rights. Auto-converts to Apache 2.0 after 4-year change date. Battle-tested (HashiCorp, MariaDB, Sentry, CockroachDB). Elastic License 2.0 rejected (too permissive). AGPL v3 rejected (deters collaboration). Four enforcement artifacts: LICENSE, COPYRIGHT, per-file headers, GPG-signed commits.

decisions.summary: Hybrid header format: GITOPS.md text, prompt extension list
decisions.rationale: Docs govern format (CLAUDE.md rule). GITOPS.md condensed single-line header is canonical. GITOPS.md omitted .sh, .css, .mjs — prompt had broader coverage. Hybrid takes the right thing from each source.

decisions.summary: Git identity: Charles K. Johnson / mobile@charleskjohnson.com
decisions.rationale: Explicit name and verified email. Used for git config, GPG key generation, GitHub verification. Aligns IP chain: COPYRIGHT -> LICENSE -> commit authorship -> GPG signature all trace to same identity.

decisions.summary: .gitattributes with text=auto eol=lf; .claude/settings.local.json tracked
decisions.rationale: .claude/settings.local.json is project-specific config inherited by Claude Code sessions — not user-specific. .gitattributes ensures consistent line endings across WSL and Windows environments. Superseded in part by D71: settings.local.json tracking assertion reversed — file is gitignored per Sprint 08.5 Track 1. .gitattributes line-endings assertion stands.

decisions.summary: SSH from WSL as canonical push method
decisions.rationale: Signing key lives in WSL. SSH push from WSL keeps sign -> commit -> push chain in one environment. Eliminates WSL/Git Bash interop requirement. Future-proofs for automated agent commits. Not yet configured — HTTPS from Git Bash is current workaround.

decisions.summary: npm as package manager
decisions.rationale: Ships with Node.js v22. No additional install or configuration. Solo project with single service doesn't need yarn/pnpm monorepo features.

decisions.summary: Fastify as Node.js API framework
decisions.rationale: TypeScript-first (aligns with strict TS standard). Built-in JSON Schema validation for webhook payloads and API contracts. Plugin system maps to modular lens architecture. Lightweight — heavy compute runs in Agents API, not on Railway.

decisions.summary: SSE for server-to-client streaming, not WebSockets
decisions.rationale: All streaming cases (triage conversation, briefing assembly, agent monitor) are server-to-client. SSE flows through Zuplo without special handling — preserves auth, rate limiting, observability. WebSockets would bypass Zuplo, losing harness layer constraints. Fastify supports SSE natively. Not needed until frontend phase.

decisions.summary: system_context as versioned document, seed reshaped
decisions.rationale: Versioned document with version, content, change_summary, active. Single active row with all K/V pairs in content JSON.

decisions.summary: Split tech inventory from tech event log
decisions.rationale: New stack_components table for static inventory. tech_watch remains event log for Phase 1 monitoring agent. Separates static inventory from temporal event stream.

decisions.summary: Dual execution runtime (Managed Agents primary, Routines as progression)
decisions.rationale: Managed Agents for complex/interactive work. Routines for bounded/scheduled. All missions start as Managed Agents. After 30 days meeting promotion criteria, missions surface for user-controlled promotion. Extended by D48: promotion gated on observability readiness.

decisions.summary: Cloudflare as DNS registrar and authoritative DNS
decisions.rationale: At-cost pricing, DNSSEC, single DNS control plane. Robust API for future programmatic DNS management. DNS-only mode (no proxy) for Zuplo and Vercel records. Data sovereignty alignment.

decisions.summary: Separate meridian-gateway repo for Zuplo configuration
decisions.rationale: Zuplo expects repo-root ownership. Different deployment targets. Prevents cross-deployment trigger noise. Same BSL 1.1 license, same conventions. Precedent followed by D72 for meridian-mcp.

decisions.summary: mydatasphere.dev as project domain
decisions.rationale: .dev TLD enforces HTTPS. Descriptive of the project scope. Clean subdomain pattern: api.mydatasphere.dev for Zuplo gateway, app.mydatasphere.dev for future Vercel frontend.

decisions.summary: Dedicated Meridian Clerk application in Personal workspace
decisions.rationale: Application isolation. Perkin Discovery Tool (existing app) transferable independently without affecting Meridian.

decisions.summary: 30-day personal PAT via Clerk JWT Template, monthly rotation
decisions.rationale: Clerk JWT Templates don't support per-token revocation — token lifetime IS the worst-case exposure window. 30-day lifetime balances security with usability. Meridian tracks mint date and expiry, sends reminder on day 25.

decisions.summary: Resend for outbound system notifications
decisions.rationale: Already in Chaz's stack. Sender domain under mydatasphere.dev with SPF/DKIM/DMARC on Cloudflare.

decisions.summary: Per-machine signing identities (SSH + GPG), never shared
decisions.rationale: Each machine maintains its own SSH key and GPG key. Independent revocation if machine lost or decommissioned. Extends to non-human actors. Initial roster: StarshipOne, Mac Mini (pending MER-31).

decisions.summary: Universal Attribution Principle
decisions.rationale: Every action that mutates state carries attribution to a specific identity. Identities never shared across humans, machines, or agents. Attribution captured at time of action, never reconstructed after the fact.

decisions.summary: M2M per-machine Clerk identity, 2hr JWT, approve-to-mint at maturity
decisions.rationale: Two-layer: Machine Secret Key (long-lived, 180-day rotation) and M2M Token (2hr session JWT). Mint-per-session. Maturity phase: notification, approve, mint flow. No silent renewals.

decisions.summary: claude.ai is planning-only; read access post-audit-layer; no stored credentials
decisions.rationale: Bootstrap: no API access. Post-audit: read-only via PAT provided per-conversation. Maturity: approve-to-mint read-only session token. Writes permanently excluded from this surface.

decisions.summary: Repos stay public (BSL 1.1); security/observability gate before new data exposure
decisions.rationale: Security posture relies on authentication (D25, D66), not obscurity. Critical sequencing gate: auth + observability must be verified operational before any new data exposed via API.

decisions.summary: meridian-api as active Clerk machine identity for outbound calls
decisions.rationale: Meridian's own outbound calls (Anthropic API, Resend, source fetches) attributed to this identity. Single identity for Railway service; segment per-function only if trust boundaries diverge later.

decisions.summary: Autonomy-Observability Cascade
decisions.rationale: System vigilance escalates in inverse proportion to human presence. Five-tier cascade. Promotion between tiers gated on proving the next tier's observability is operational.

decisions.summary: Local cold storage primary, encrypted cloud DR only
decisions.rationale: Primary cold storage is local external drive under physical control. Meridian tracks storage topology. Cloud (R2) is encrypted DR only with owner-controlled key.

decisions.summary: Pino + ULID trace IDs; OpenTelemetry deferred to agent fan-out
decisions.rationale: Linear trace served by ULID correlation. OTel adopted when agent fan-out creates multi-service traces requiring distributed tracing.

decisions.summary: Inbox-posture bootstrap alerts via Resend; 5 alert types
decisions.rationale: Auth failure spike, unknown caller, write outside session, PAT expiry, Machine Secret Key expiry. Email-based, not pager. Bootstrap-level alerting for pre-agent operational awareness.

decisions.summary: audit_events and agent_events separate, trace_id-linked
decisions.rationale: Different cardinality, retention, primary concerns. Trace_id links them. Independent audit streams, reconcilable but not collapsed.

decisions.summary: Data Sovereignty at Rest
decisions.rationale: Data at rest defaults to sovereign local storage. Cloud storage is encrypted DR only with owner-controlled key. Scope clarified by D61: sovereign sources stay local, operational/derived may be cloud-hosted because reconstructable.

decisions.summary: Observability as Knowledge Infrastructure
decisions.rationale: Observability data is first-class knowledge, not a security byproduct. Every observability event has two consumers: security/audit path and knowledge/context path. Both served from the same capture.

decisions.summary: Deep Context as Standard Capture Protocol
decisions.rationale: Three-tier capture model: event capture per-action, session synthesis per-session-close, deep context per-planning-session. Every deep context carries a structured YAML frontmatter metadata header.

decisions.summary: Sprint 08.5 inserted (two-track structure)
decisions.rationale: Track 1 (Claude Code reconciliation: migration 004 fixes, secrets infrastructure, doc updates) + Track 2 (vendor confirmations: Clerk, Zuplo, Railway, Cloudflare). Sprint 09 Phase 1 hotfix does not ship until both tracks close.

decisions.summary: Sprint 10 split into 10a and 10b
decisions.rationale: 10a: Orchestration + Audit Foundation. 10b: Notifications + Cold Storage + Alerts + Query. One-week burn-in between. Audit layer proves itself operational before downstream work depends on it.

decisions.summary: MER-29 (deep-context skill update per D55) moves to Sprint 08.5 Track 1
decisions.rationale: Forward-compliance with D55 on every future session close. Skill file is canonical protocol implementation; hours-scale work, should happen before next unsupervised session.

decisions.summary: Attribution via identities FK registry, full attribution record
decisions.rationale: Every action-recording table carries actor_identity_id FK to identities registry. Full record per action: actor_identity_id, endpoint, environment, host, ip_address, occurred_at. Initial seed: system:anonymous, machine:starshipone-claude-code, machine:meridian-api, human:chaz-clerk-pat.

decisions.summary: Sibling observability_readiness table for D48 promotion evaluation
decisions.rationale: Two-table promotion check: promotion_thresholds (behavioral) AND observability_readiness (observational). Sibling table allows tier-specific readiness profiles. Permissive defaults until Sprint 10a populates real readiness rows.

decisions.summary: Railway operational/derived; local disk sovereign source; no inversion
decisions.rationale: Meridian's sovereign data is source material (emails, documents) on local disk. Railway holds derived and operational data — all reconstructable from sources. D53 canon satisfied by existing design; no topology inversion required.

decisions.summary: Two Clerk JWT templates: meridian-human-pat and meridian-machine-m2m
decisions.rationale: 30-day lifetime on human-pat (D40); 2-hour on machine-m2m (D44). Independent signing keys. Shared template means emergency rotation of one class kills the other — self-inflicted agent outage on every human credential scare.

decisions.summary: Approval endpoints require session JWT; reject PAT
decisions.rationale: Approval routes validate session JWT only. D48 Tier 2 human-approved derives meaning from presence at approval time; stored PAT cannot prove presence.

decisions.summary: /health public narrow; /v1/system Clerk-authenticated
decisions.rationale: /health returns status ok/degraded only. Public, exempt from shared-secret middleware for Railway deploy probe. /v1/system behind Zuplo Clerk JWT Auth policy with rich body. Triggered D66 canon elevation.

decisions.summary: Branch protection after Sprint 09 Phase 2 (SSH verified)
decisions.rationale: Enable on both repos after Phase 2 SSH work closes. Rules: require signed commits, require linear history, disable force pushes, disable deletions, disable administrator bypass. D25 already voluntarily honored; platform enforcement tightens.

decisions.summary: Universal API Authentication
decisions.rationale: Every API endpoint that returns or mutates data requires authentication. No data-returning API surface is ever anonymously accessible. Shared-secret layer is transport integrity, not authentication. Exception list: /health only for Meridian.

decisions.summary: Webhook HMAC validation at Zuplo (Cloudflare Workers fallback)
decisions.rationale: Zuplo validates inbound webhook HMAC signatures as a policy on webhook routes, attributes to machine:<provider>-webhook per D43. Fallback: Cloudflare Workers edge validator if Zuplo reveals limitations.

decisions.summary: Dual-surface architecture adopted; edge/mobile deferred
decisions.rationale: Control plane (api.mydatasphere.dev via Zuplo/Railway with Clerk auth). Edge/mobile surface deferred. Credential boundary: edge surface does NOT mint own long-lived credentials. Reactivation triggers defined.

decisions.summary: Seed default ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE for mutable columns
decisions.rationale: Flip from ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING default. Immutable columns preserved. Mutable columns update. --reseed stays as dev-only truncate path. New --dry-run flag prints changes without writing.

decisions.summary: canon BOOLEAN column on decisions table + docs/CANON.md mirror
decisions.rationale: Queryable canon flag per master CLAUDE.md. Boolean matches binary semantic. docs/CANON.md is human-readable mirror of canon subset. Seed back-fill: D03, D43, D48, D53, D54, D55, D66 = true.

decisions.summary: .claude/settings.local.json untracked (supersedes D28 in part)
decisions.rationale: git rm --cached .claude/settings.local.json in Sprint 08.5 Track 1. Three sessions of observed churn confirm machine-local state, not project-shared config. D28 .gitattributes assertion stands; tracking assertion reverses.

decisions.summary: MCP architecture: own codebase (meridian-mcp), transport-abstracted, staggered deployment
decisions.rationale: Drop Zuplo MCP Server Handler dependency. Build meridian-mcp as standalone using official MCP TypeScript SDK. Two entry points: stdio (local) and http (remote). D06 scope narrowed: Zuplo is HTTP gateway only, MCP hosting is separate.

decisions.summary: /secrets/ gitignored directory + three-layer enforcement
decisions.rationale: Directory-level gitignore catches future secret-adjacent artifacts. Three enforcement layers: .gitignore, pre-commit hook check-secrets.sh, GitHub custom secret-scanning pattern. Split docs: /secrets/SECRETS.md (gitignored, pointer registry) + docs/SECRETS_POLICY.md (committed, public-facing).

decisions.summary: Emergency rotation procedures per credential class
decisions.rationale: Break-glass procedures documented in /secrets/SECRETS.md. Specific procedures for Clerk PAT, Clerk machine secret, webhook HMAC, GATEWAY_SECRET, DATABASE_URL. Compromised identities retired not reused. Brief outages acceptable; continuing with compromised credentials is not.

issues.description: AGE extension deployment via custom Docker image from apache/age not yet validated. Dockerfile not written. Must validate: AGE extension loads, Cypher queries execute, shared_preload_libraries configured, connection pool handles per-connection LOAD 'age' requirement.
issues.resolution: Deferred to Phase 2. Phase 0 uses plain PostgreSQL without AGE extension.

issues.description: Node types, edge types, domain vectors, property conventions for the canonical knowledge graph.
issues.resolution: 9 node types, 13 edge types, 4 domain vectors. Schema document: docs/SCHEMA.md

issues.description: Full pipeline from email file to graph write. Extraction prompt done, staging table designed, triage protocol conceptual. Python ingestion adapter not built.
issues.resolution: Pipeline changed from extract->validate->write to extract->validate->stage->human review->write per D24.

issues.description: Zuplo project creation, API route definitions for lens endpoints, MCP Server Handler configuration, caching and rate limiting policies. Needs model routing parameter support per D22.
issues.summary: Zuplo project for Meridian not created
issues.resolution: Blocked by: no API endpoints to expose until API service is scaffolded.

issues.description: How the email archive is made available for ingestion pipeline.
issues.resolution: .eml/.html pairs from M365 Graph API export. Direct file system read from D:\email archive\m365\. POC subset: 2024-2025 (143,996 files, 8.74 GB).

issues.description: Test extraction prompt against actual emails from the 2024-2025 subset across multiple domains and source types.
issues.summary: Extraction prompt validation against real corpus
issues.resolution: 9 emails tested across 5 source types, 3 domains. Quality strong for entity/edge identification. Failures in domain classification and entity resolution — addressable via prompt updates and post-processing.

issues.description: Same person appearing across hundreds of emails with name variations and multiple email addresses. The pipeline's hardest problem.
issues.resolution: Concrete evidence from Session 04: Marcie Rodriguez has two email addresses at same domain (marcie.rodriguez@humbleisd.net vs marodrig@humbleisd.net). Reframed as part of MER-09.

issues.description: Five changes documented in Session 04 not yet applied to docs/EXTRACTION.md: org_type expansion (school, school_district), abbreviation verbatim rule, name-from-email parsing heuristic, calendar invite source_type clarification, travel dual-tag default.
issues.summary: Extraction prompt v1.2 changes not applied
issues.resolution: docs/EXTRACTION.md is still v1. Changes documented in Session 04 deep context.

issues.description: Beyond email-as-primary-key. Need: (a) email match, (b) display name + domain match, (c) fuzzy name match within same organization. Marcie Rodriguez case is the concrete test case.
issues.resolution: Same person, two addresses at same domain. Email-key merge will fail without secondary matching.

issues.description: Formal spec for three-function model (Conversational Triage, Insight Explorer, Execution Layer), recommendations engine, model selection settings, staging-to-graph-write flow.
issues.resolution: Formalized in SAD Section 10.

issues.description: PostgreSQL table for holding extractions before human review. Schema includes: extraction_id, email_message_id, extracted_json, status enum, reviewer_notes, reviewed_at, batch_id, corrections_applied jsonb, model_used.
issues.resolution: Fully designed in SAD Section 7.1.4 and docs/DATABASE.md. DDL ready.

issues.description: Haiku periodic analysis against graph -> candidate generation -> queue for presentation. Trigger conditions, frequency, candidate schema, dismissal tracking all undefined.
issues.resolution: Deferred to Phase 4.

issues.description: SAD was produced as .docx for formal distribution. Claude Code can't read .docx. Need markdown equivalent at docs/SAD_v1.0.md.
issues.resolution: Deferred.

issues.description: Phase 0 Git and GitHub preparation: GPG signing, LICENSE, COPYRIGHT, README, .gitignore, .gitattributes, header templates, pre-commit hook, first signed commit.
issues.resolution: Executed in Session 06. Two signed commits: cadddfc (Phase 0 foundation) and 2086bc9 (routing actions). Both verified on GitHub.

issues.description: Agents API primitives (agents, environments, sessions, events) shared as structured table but source not verified against Anthropic published documentation. Architecture works with Claude Code subagents and Railway scheduled jobs as interim if not GA.
issues.resolution: Check Anthropic docs for GA status.

issues.description: Source manager SPA built but no actual sources entered. YouTube channels, RSS feeds, subreddits, and other monitored sources need to be entered. Prerequisite for agent mission definitions.
issues.resolution: SPA uses browser persistent storage. Will be replaced by API-backed version.

issues.description: Fastify + TypeScript strict. Project structure per CODING_STANDARDS.md. Connection pool with AGE init pattern. Phase 0 endpoints for project state queries.
issues.resolution: Framework (Fastify) and package manager (npm) decided in Session 07.

issues.description: D29 documents SSH from WSL as canonical push method. ssh-keygen not yet run. Public key not added to GitHub. Remote not switched from HTTPS to SSH.
issues.summary: SSH key not configured on WSL
issues.resolution: Deferred in Session 07. Current HTTPS push from Git Bash is functional. Not blocking development.

issues.description: WSL has gitsome (a different Python tool) instead of real GitHub CLI. GitHub API operations require Git Bash. Consider installing real gh in WSL via apt.
issues.summary: gh CLI not available in WSL
issues.resolution: Low priority. Git Bash workaround exists.

issues.description: Five low-severity insights from Session 07 pending routing review.
DATABASE.md maintenance burden growing. Need docs/OPERATIONS.md for runbooks, alert response, cold storage procedures.
MCP Server Handler on Zuplo — original plan for MCP exposure via Zuplo gateway.
issues.resolution: Won't-do per D72. Architecturally redirected to meridian-mcp.

issues.description: Seed data does not include decisions D33-D74, sessions 07-11, or issue statuses from recon sessions.

issues.summary: D29 vs reality - SSH configuration on WSL
issues.description: SSH from WSL (D29) not yet configured. HTTPS from Git Bash remains the workaround.
Session 08 insight ledger (6 insights, INS-001 through INS-006) pending routing review and action.

issues.summary: API live and unauthenticated - shared-secret hotfix
issues.description: Meridian API is live on Railway and accessible without any authentication. Shared-secret middleware hotfix required before any new data exposure.
Railway direct URL accessible without going through Zuplo gateway. Direct Railway URL access is a security violation.
Resend sending domain under mydatasphere.dev not yet verified. SPF/DKIM/DMARC records needed.

issues.summary: Deep context skill update per D55
issues.description: Deep context skill file needs update to enforce D55 structured metadata header (YAML frontmatter) requirements.
issues.resolution: Merged with D58; deep-context skill update scheduled Sprint 08.5 Track 1

issues.description: Verify that Clerk Pro plan supports M2M token capabilities needed for D44 machine identity architecture.

issues.summary: Mac Mini provisioning checklist (urgency elevated by D65)
issues.description: Mac Mini needs SSH key, GPG key, Clerk M2M identity, and Claude Code setup. Urgency elevated by D65 branch protection (need emergency-recovery signing identity).

issues.summary: D11 supersession by D22 not reflected in seed
issues.description: D11 superseded by D22 but seed data may not accurately reflect the supersession chain.
Migration 004 on disk is missing BEGIN/COMMIT transaction wrapper and schema_migrations INSERT statement.

issues.summary: Migration 004 IF NOT EXISTS + D43 attribution per D59
issues.description: Migration 004 missing IF NOT EXISTS guards and D43 attribution columns (actor_identity_id FK) per D59 design.

issues.summary: Clerk JWT-template PAT validation spike vs Zuplo
issues.description: Validate that Clerk JWT template for human PAT (meridian-human-pat) works with Zuplo Clerk JWT Auth policy.

issues.summary: Clerk M2M token validation spike vs Zuplo
issues.description: Validate that Clerk M2M token (meridian-machine-m2m) works with Zuplo Clerk JWT Auth policy.

issues.summary: Fastify middleware ordering + rejection trace coverage
issues.description: Fastify middleware ordering needs design to ensure shared-secret, Clerk auth, and audit layers execute in correct sequence with rejection trace coverage.
Scaffold /secrets/ directory, SECRETS.md pointer registry, docs/SECRETS_POLICY.md, and three-layer enforcement per D73.

issues.summary: Two Clerk JWT templates per D62
issues.description: Create two Clerk JWT templates (meridian-human-pat and meridian-machine-m2m) with independent signing keys per D62.
.env.example file documenting required environment variables not yet committed to repo.
railway.toml configuration file not yet committed to repo.
CORS middleware with strict origin allowlist needed for API security.
Narrow /health response body to prevent fingerprinting. Add /v1/system endpoint behind Clerk auth with rich diagnostic body per D64.
Dead-letter path for audit events that fail to write. Audit-of-audit ensures audit failures are themselves observable.
Verify that Zuplo rate-limit policies can extract identity from Clerk JWT for per-identity rate limiting.

issues.summary: Seed ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE per D69
issues.description: Update seed script to use ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE for mutable columns per D69.
observability_readiness table structure with permissive defaults for Sprint 08.5; real readiness rows populated in Sprint 10a when audit layer is operational.
Ambiguity about whether D53 requires moving the live database off Railway to local storage.
issues.resolution: Resolved by D61. Railway operational + local sovereign satisfies canon without inversion.

issues.summary: D54 agent_events to AGE graph ingest path
issues.description: Design and build ingest path from audit_events and agent_sessions JSONB columns into AGE graph per D54.
Deep context skill update for D55 compliance.
issues.resolution: Duplicate of MER-29; merged.

issues.summary: Canon back-fill (D33-D74 + canon flag per D70)
issues.description: Back-fill seed data with decisions D33-D74 and canon boolean flag on all decisions per D70.
Capability manifest endpoint for agent/tool discovery.
Continuous integration pipeline for automated testing and build verification.
Standardize insight ledger filenames to include session number prefix.
Document identity bootstrapping procedures in docs/SECRETS_POLICY.md.

issues.summary: Railway private networking - if available, Layer A obsolete
issues.description: Investigate if Railway private networking is available. If so, the shared-secret Layer A becomes obsolete.
Document D06 scope narrowing per D72 (Zuplo is HTTP gateway only, not MCP host).

issues.summary: D48 retention behavior on session-promoted audit rows
issues.description: Design detail for D48 retention behavior when audit rows are promoted across session boundaries.
Determine webhook validation surface (Zuplo vs Cloudflare Workers).
issues.resolution: Resolved by D67. Zuplo HMAC primary, CF Workers fallback.

issues.summary: meridian-mcp repo scaffold + Variant A deployment per D72
issues.description: Scaffold meridian-mcp repository and deploy Variant A (local subprocess) on dev machines per D72.
MCP Variant B (remote HTTP service) deferred until concrete use case: first managed agent mission, mobile client, or external integration.

issues.summary: GitHub custom secret-scanning pattern per D73
issues.description: Configure GitHub custom secret-scanning pattern meridian-secrets-directory with Critical severity and push protection on both active repos.
Create pre-commit hook check-secrets.sh that rejects any staged file under secrets/ path per D73.

legacy_issues.summary: Architecture decision between pure CAG and GraphRAG
legacy_issues.resolution: Resolved by D04. Neither in isolation. Unified 'one store, many lenses' architecture adopted.

legacy_issues.summary: Entity extraction quality against real email corpus
legacy_issues.resolution: Resolved by MER-06. Extraction quality validated in Session 04.
Reframed as compiled views lens within unified architecture.
Skill active and triggering correctly as of Session 02.

sessions.theme: Architecture evaluation - CAG vs RAG vs GraphRAG, deep-context skill creation
sessions.summary: Project inception. Evaluated CAG, RAG, and GraphRAG architectures for personal AI OS built on ~300K email corpus. CAG selected as primary candidate. GraphRAG as hybrid alternative. Deep context methodology formalized as reusable skill from Perkin Session 33 reference.

sessions.theme: Architecture refinement - unified store, Zuplo integration, graph DB selection, project naming, repo creation
sessions.summary: Reframed from pick-one to unified architecture. Multi-backend + router model critiqued (7 weaknesses). Replaced with 'one store, many lenses' architecture. PostgreSQL+AGE selected as sole database. Kuzu eliminated (Apple acquisition). Zuplo as API/MCP layer. Project named Meridian. Repo created.
artifacts_produced: Unified knowledge architecture diagram, GitHub repository (ckj9779/Meridian)

sessions.theme: Graph schema design, four-domain model, entity extraction prompt, email corpus format discovery
sessions.summary: Designed graph schema: 9 node types, 13 edge types, 4 domain vectors. Email corpus confirmed as .eml/.html pairs from M365 Graph API export. No pre-filter decision. Model-agnostic schema via JSON contract.
artifacts_produced: Graph schema v1 (docs/SCHEMA.md), extraction prompt v1.1 (docs/EXTRACTION.md), schema overview diagram

sessions.theme: Extraction prompt validation against real email data, interface architecture - three-function conversational model
sessions.summary: Tested extraction against 9 real emails spanning 5 source types and 3 domains. Pivotal observation: extraction is proposal, not fact (D24). Three-function interface designed. Model selection moved to user preference (D22).
artifacts_produced: 8 extraction validations, extraction prompt v1.2 change spec, interface architecture concept

sessions.theme: Agentic intelligence architecture, System Architecture Document, licensing/IP framework
sessions.summary: Mapped full email ecosystem. Intelligence gathering identified as primary value proposition. Six-layer architecture formalized. BSL 1.1 license selected. SAD produced. CLAUDE.md and modular docs ecosystem created.
artifacts_produced: Intelligence source manager SPA, system architecture diagram, SAD v1.0, CLAUDE.md, 4 developer reference docs

sessions.theme: Insight protocol design, CLAUDE.md rewrite, Git/GitHub Phase 0 setup via Claude Code
sessions.summary: Designed insight protocol with eight insight types and routing targets. CLAUDE.md rewritten. Two Claude Code prompts executed. First two signed commits verified on GitHub. GPG key generated.
artifacts_produced: CLAUDE.md full rewrite, 2 Claude Code prompts, 2 signed commits, 2 insight ledgers, 14 insights captured

sessions.theme: Phase 0 infrastructure - API framework, package manager, streaming, system context, tech inventory
sessions.summary: Selected npm as package manager, Fastify as API framework, SSE for streaming. Reshaped system_context as versioned document. Split tech inventory from tech event log with new stack_components table.
artifacts_produced: Phase 0 API scaffold, migration 003, seed data, DATABASE.md updates

sessions.theme: Phase 0 completion - execution runtime, DNS, domain, gateway repo
sessions.summary: Adopted dual execution runtime (Managed Agents primary, Routines as progression). Selected Cloudflare as DNS registrar. Created separate meridian-gateway repo for Zuplo. Established mydatasphere.dev as project domain.
artifacts_produced: meridian-gateway repo, Zuplo proxy routes, Cloudflare DNS configuration

sessions.theme: Security, observability, canon governance - Clerk, attribution, observability cascade, data sovereignty
sessions.summary: Established 17 decisions covering security architecture, observability, and canon governance. Five principles elevated to cross-project canon (D43, D48, D53, D54, D55). Clerk application, PAT/M2M identity architecture, alerting, audit design, and data sovereignty all locked.
artifacts_produced: Master CLAUDE.md v1.0, canon governance protocol, 5 canon principles

sessions.theme: Sprint plan diagnostic recon - 56 insights, 26 candidate issues
sessions.summary: Diagnostic recon session. No decisions locked. 56 insights captured, 26 candidate issues identified. Findings routed to Session 11 for decision lockdown.

sessions.theme: Decision lockdown - 18 decisions (D56-D74), D66 canon elevation
sessions.summary: Locked 18 decisions (D56-D74). D66 (Universal API Authentication) elevated to seventh canon principle. Master CLAUDE.md updated to v1.1. Sprint 08.5 two-track structure established. Migration 004 reconciliation designed.
artifacts_produced: Master CLAUDE.md v1.1, project CLAUDE.md v1.1, D66 canon principle, 18 locked decisions

system_context.value: Can remember - PostgreSQL + API + Zuplo MCP
One store, many lenses - unified canonical knowledge graph with three retrieval lenses
PostgreSQL + Apache AGE (AGE deferred to Phase 2)
Zuplo - auth, rate limiting, semantic caching, MCP exposure
HTTPS from Git Bash (workaround). SSH from WSL planned (D29, MER-18 deferred).
Four domains: Professional, Private, Spiritual, Romantic
Intelligence multiplier - graph-scored briefings from automated source scanning. Compresses 5-10 hrs/day manual intelligence gathering to 30-60 min reviewed briefings.
415,627 files, 126 folders, 26.3 GB (~200K emails). POC subset 2024-2025: 143,996 files, 8.74 GB (~72K emails).
90%+ personal. Four-domain data. Not yet exported.

model_preferences.notes: Cost-effective for high-volume email extraction. Validated in Session 04.
Default for triage conversations, insight exploration, general reasoning.
Complex queries, multi-hop graph reasoning, briefing synthesis.
Periodic graph analysis for recommendation generation. Cost-sensitive.

tech_watch.notes: Cypher query support. Deferred to Phase 2. Custom Docker image from apache/age planned.
Auth, rate limiting, semantic caching, MCP exposure, observability. Edge deployment.
PostgreSQL deployed and online. Meridian project created.
TypeScript-first. JSON Schema validation. Plugin architecture. Selected in Session 07.
Phase 0 tables only. AGE extension deferred to Phase 2.
MER-15: GA status unconfirmed. Interim: Claude Code subagents + Railway scheduled jobs.
Frontend only. Not for backend services (D07).
